topics: Activities: To measure the resistance and impedance of an inductor with or without iron core.
Activities: To measure resistance, voltage (AC/DC), current (AC) and check continuity of a given circuit using multimeter.
Activities: To assemble a household circuit comprising three bulbs, three (on/off) switches, a fuse and a power source.
Activities: To assemble the components of a given electrical circuit.
Activities: To study the variation in potential drop with length of a wire for a steady current.

Activities: To draw the diagram of a given open circuit comprising at least a battery, resistor/rheostat, key, ammeter and voltmeter. Mark the components that are not connected in proper order and correct the circuit and also the circuit diagram.
Experiments SECTION–B: To find the value of v for different values of u in case of a concave mirror and to find the focal length.
Experiments SECTION–B: To find the focal length of a convex mirror, using a convex lens.
Experiments SECTION–B: To find the focal length of a convex lens by plotting graphs between u and v or between 1/u and 1/v .
Experiments SECTION–B: To find the focal length of a concave lens, using a convex lens.
Experiments SECTION–B: To determine angle of minimum deviation for a given prism by plotting a graph between angle of incidence and angle of deviation.
Experiments SECTION–B: To determine refractive index of a glass slab using a travelling microscope.
Experiments SECTION–B: To find the refractive index of a liquid using convex lens and plane mirror.
Experiments SECTION–B: To find the refractive index of a liquid using a concave mirror and a plane mirror.
Experiments SECTION–B: To draw the I-V characteristic curve for a p-n junction diode in forward and reverse bias.
Activities SECTION–B: To identify a diode, an LED, a resistor and a capacitor from a mixed collection of such items.
Activities SECTION–B: Use of multimeter to see the unidirectional flow of current in case of a diode and an LED and check whether a given electronic component (e.g., diode) is in working order.
Activities SECTION–B: To study effect of intensity of light (by varying distance of the source) on an LDR.
Activities SECTION–B: To observe refraction and lateral deviation of a beam of light incident obliquely on a glass slab.
Activities SECTION–B: To observe diffraction of light due to a thin slit.

Activities SECTION–B: To study the nature and size of the image formed by a (i) convex lens, or (ii) concave mirror, on a screen by using a candle and a screen (for different distances of the candle from the lens/mirror).
Activities SECTION–B: To obtain a lens combination with the specified focal length by using two lenses from the given set of lenses.
subtopics: Suggested Investigatory Projects: To study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends.

Suggested Investigatory Projects: To study the variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR because of a variation in (a) the power of the incandescent lamp, used to 'illuminate' the LDR (keeping all the lamps at a fixed distance). (b) the distance of an incandescent lamp (of fixed power) used to 'illuminate' the LDR.

Suggested Investigatory Projects: To find the refractive indices of (a) water (b) oil (transparent) using a plane mirror, an equiconvex lens (made from a glass of known refractive index) and an adjustable object needle.

Suggested Investigatory Projects: To investigate the relation between the ratio of (i) output and input voltage and (ii) number of turns in the secondary coil and primary coil of a self-designed transformer.
Suggested Investigatory Projects: To investigate the dependence of the angle of deviation on the angle of incidence using a hollow prism filled one by one, with different transparent fluids.
Suggested Investigatory Projects: To estimate the charge induced on each one of the two identical Styrofoam (or pith) balls suspended in a vertical plane by making use of Coulomb's law.

Suggested Investigatory Projects: To study the factor on which the self-inductance of a coil depends by observing the effect of this coil, when put in series with a resistor/(bulb) in a circuit fed up by an A.C. source of adjustable frequency.
Suggested Investigatory Projects: To study the earth's magnetic field using a compass needle -bar magnet by plotting magnetic field lines and tangent galvanometer.

topics: Record of at least 8 Experiments [with 4 from each section], to be performed by the students.
Record of at least 6 Activities [with 3 each from section A and section B], to be performed by the students.
The Report of the project carried out by the students.
Evaluation Scheme: Max. Marks: 30 Time 3 hours
Two experiments one from each section 7+7 Marks
Practical record [experiments and activities] 5 Marks
One activity from any section 3 Marks
Investigatory Project 3 Marks
Viva on experiments, activities and project 5 Marks
Total 30 marks
subtopics: Experiments SECTION–A: To determine resistivity of two / three wires by plotting a graph for potential difference versus current.
Experiments SECTION–A: To find resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using metre bridge.
Experiments SECTION–A: To verify the laws of combination (series) of resistances using a metre bridge.
Experiments SECTION–A: To verify the laws of combination (parallel) of resistances using a metre bridge.
Experiments SECTION–A: To determine resistance of a galvanometer by half-deflection method and to find its figure of merit.
Experiments SECTION–A: To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into a voltmeter of desired range and to verify the same.
Experiments SECTION–A: To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into an ammeter of desired range and to verify the same.
Experiments SECTION–A: To find the frequency of AC mains with a sonometer.

practicals.title: To determine the resistance per cm of a given wire by plotting a graph between voltage and current
practicals.details: Determine resistance per cm using V-I graph.

practicals.title: To verify the laws of combination (series/parallel combination) of resistances by Ohm's law
practicals.details: Verify laws of resistance combination using Ohm's law.

practicals.title: To find the resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using a meter bridge
practicals.details: Find resistance using meter bridge.

practicals.title: To determine the resistance of a galvanometer by half deflection method
practicals.unit_name: Unit III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
practicals.details: Determine galvanometer resistance using half deflection method.

practicals.title: To identify a resistor, capacitor, inductor and diode from a mixed collection of such items
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
practicals.details: Identify electronic components from a mixed collection.

practicals.title: To observe the difference between (i) a convex lens and a concave lens (ii) a convex mirror and a concave mirror and to estimate the likely difference between the power of two given convex /concave lenses
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
practicals.details: Observe differences between lens types and mirrors and estimate power differences.

practicals.title: To design an inductor coil and to know the effect of (i) change in the number of turns (ii) Introduction of ferromagnetic material as its core material on the inductance of the coil
practicals.unit_name: Unit IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
practicals.details: Design inductor coil and study effect of turns and core material.

practicals.title: To design a (i) step up (ii) step down transformer on a given core and know the relation between its input and output voltages
practicals.unit_name: Unit IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
practicals.details: Design transformers and determine voltage relations.

practicals.title: To determine resistivity of two / three wires by plotting a graph for potential difference versus current
practicals.details: Determine resistivity using P-D vs I graph.

practicals.title: To find resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using metre bridge
practicals.details: Find resistance using metre bridge.

practicals.title: To verify the laws of combination (series) of resistances using a metre bridge
practicals.details: Verify series resistance laws using metre bridge.

practicals.title: To verify the laws of combination (parallel) of resistances using a metre bridge
practicals.details: Verify parallel resistance laws using metre bridge.

practicals.title: To determine resistance of a galvanometer by half-deflection method and to find its figure of merit
practicals.unit_name: Unit III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
practicals.details: Determine galvanometer resistance and figure of merit.

practicals.title: To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into a voltmeter of desired range and to verify the same
practicals.unit_name: Unit III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
practicals.details: Convert galvanometer to voltmeter.

practicals.title: To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into an ammeter of desired range and to verify the same
practicals.unit_name: Unit III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
practicals.details: Convert galvanometer to ammeter.

practicals.title: To find the frequency of AC mains with a sonometer
practicals.unit_name: Unit IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
practicals.details: Find AC mains frequency using sonometer.

practicals.title: To find the value of v for different values of u in case of a concave mirror and to find the focal length
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
practicals.details: Find focal length of concave mirror.

practicals.title: To find the focal length of a convex mirror, using a convex lens
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
practicals.details: Find focal length of convex mirror using convex lens.

practicals.title: To find the focal length of a convex lens by plotting graphs between u and v or between 1/u and 1/v
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
practicals.details: Find focal length of convex lens using graphs.

practicals.title: To find the focal length of a concave lens, using a convex lens
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
practicals.details: Find focal length of concave lens using convex lens.

practicals.title: To determine angle of minimum deviation for a given prism by plotting a graph between angle of incidence and angle of deviation
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
practicals.details: Determine angle of minimum deviation using graph.

practicals.title: To determine refractive index of a glass slab using a travelling microscope
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
practicals.details: Determine refractive index of glass slab.

practicals.title: To find the refractive index of a liquid using convex lens and plane mirror
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
practicals.details: Find refractive index of liquid.

practicals.title: To find the refractive index of a liquid using a concave mirror and a plane mirror
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
practicals.details: Find refractive index of liquid.

practicals.title: To draw the I-V characteristic curve for a p-n junction diode in forward and reverse bias
practicals.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
practicals.details: Draw I-V characteristic curve for p-n junction diode.

activities.title: To measure the resistance and impedance of an inductor with or without iron core
activities.unit_name: Unit IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
activities.details: Measure resistance and impedance of an inductor with or without iron core.

activities.title: To measure resistance, voltage (AC/DC), current (AC) and check continuity of a given circuit using multimeter
activities.details: Measure resistance, voltage, current and check continuity using multimeter.

activities.title: To assemble a household circuit comprising three bulbs, three (on/off) switches, a fuse and a power source
activities.details: Assemble a household circuit with specified components.

activities.title: To assemble the components of a given electrical circuit
activities.details: Assemble components of a given electrical circuit.

activities.title: To study the variation in potential drop with length of a wire for a steady current
activities.details: Study variation in potential drop with length of a wire.

activities.title: To draw the diagram of a given open circuit comprising at least a battery, resistor/rheostat, key, ammeter and voltmeter. Mark the components that are not connected in proper order and correct the circuit and also the circuit diagram
activities.details: Draw and correct an open circuit diagram.

activities.title: To identify a diode, an LED, a resistor and a capacitor from a mixed collection of such items
activities.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
activities.details: Identify electronic components from a mixed collection.

activities.title: Use of multimeter to see the unidirectional flow of current in case of a diode and an LED and check whether a given electronic component (e.g., diode) is in working order
activities.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
activities.details: Use multimeter to check unidirectional flow and working order of components.

activities.title: To study effect of intensity of light (by varying distance of the source) on an LDR
activities.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
activities.details: Study effect of light intensity on an LDR.

activities.title: To observe refraction and lateral deviation of a beam of light incident obliquely on a glass slab
activities.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
activities.details: Observe refraction and lateral deviation of light.

activities.title: To observe diffraction of light due to a thin slit
activities.details: Observe diffraction of light due to a thin slit.

activities.title: To study the nature and size of the image formed by a (i) convex lens, or (ii) concave mirror, on a screen by using a candle and a screen (for different distances of the candle from the lens/mirror)
activities.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
activities.details: Study nature and size of image formed by lenses or mirrors.

activities.title: To obtain a lens combination with the specified focal length by using two lenses from the given set of lenses
activities.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
activities.details: Obtain a lens combination with specified focal length.

activities.title: To identify a resistor, capacitor, inductor and diode from a mixed collection of such items
activities.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
activities.details: Identify electronic components from a mixed collection.

activities.title: To observe the difference between (i) a convex lens and a concave lens (ii) a convex mirror and a concave mirror and to estimate the likely difference between the power of two given convex /concave lenses
activities.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
activities.details: Observe differences between lens types and mirrors and estimate power differences.

projects.title: To study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends
projects.details: Study factors affecting internal resistance and EMF of a cell.

projects.title: To study the variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR because of a variation in (a) the power of the incandescent lamp, used to 'illuminate' the LDR (keeping all the lamps at a fixed distance). (b) the distance of an incandescent lamp (of fixed power) used to 'illuminate' the LDR
projects.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
projects.details: Study variations in current due to changes in lamp power or distance affecting an LDR.

projects.title: To find the refractive indices of (a) water (b) oil (transparent) using a plane mirror, an equiconvex lens (made from a glass of known refractive index) and an adjustable object needle
projects.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
projects.details: Find refractive indices of water and oil using specific optical setup.

projects.title: To investigate the relation between the ratio of (i) output and input voltage and (ii) number of turns in the secondary coil and primary coil of a self-designed transformer
projects.unit_name: Unit IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
projects.details: Investigate relation between voltage ratio, turn ratio, and transformer design.

projects.title: To investigate the dependence of the angle of deviation on the angle of incidence using a hollow prism filled one by one, with different transparent fluids
projects.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
projects.details: Investigate dependence of angle of deviation on angle of incidence using hollow prism.

projects.title: To estimate the charge induced on each one of the two identical Styrofoam (or pith) balls suspended in a vertical plane by making use of Coulomb's law
projects.details: Estimate induced charge using Coulomb's law.

projects.title: To study the factor on which the self-inductance of a coil depends by observing the effect of this coil, when put in series with a resistor/(bulb) in a circuit fed up by an A.C. source of adjustable frequency
projects.unit_name: Unit IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
projects.details: Study factors affecting self-inductance using AC circuit.

projects.title: To study the earth's magnetic field using a compass needle -bar magnet by plotting magnetic field lines and tangent galvanometer
projects.unit_name: Unit III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
projects.details: Study earth's magnetic field using compass and tangent galvanometer.
